chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P. No.D-1983 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE For order on office objection. For hearing of main case. 26.03.2026 Mr. Maria Bughio advocate for petitioners along with petitioners No.1to3 and 5. Mr. Kamaluddin advocate for respondents No.5to7/University. Mr. Muhammad Ismail Bhutto, Additional A.G. Sindh. The petitioners No.1to3 and 5 present have filed their affidavits of apology, which are taken on record. Copy whereof provided to the learned counsel for the respondents No.5to7/University, who after going through the same raises no objection and by consent seek disposal in the terms of affidavits of apology. By consent, instant petition is disposed of in the terms as per paras-6, 7 & 8 of affidavits filed by the petitioners which read as under:- “6. That I hereby solemnly undertake before this Honourable Court that I shall strictly observe and abide by all the rules, regulations, and the code of conduct prescribed by the University administration. 7. That I do hereby apology to the University. I further undertake that, henceforth, I shall not involve myself in any activity, protest, or act of indiscipline that may disturb the academic atmosphere, decorum, or peaceful environment of the University, no shall I engage in any such behaviour that violates the University’s policies. 8. That I am fully aware that any breach of this undertaking shall render me liable to face strict disciplinary action by the University authorities, including the possibility of expulsion or any other legal proceedings deemed appropriate by the competent authority or this Honourable Court.” Instant petition stands disposed of in the above terms.

I further undertake that, henceforth, I shall not involve myself in any activity, protest, or act of indiscipline that may disturb the academic atmosphere, decorum, or peaceful environment of the University, no shall I engage in any such behaviour that violates the University’s policies. 8. That I am fully aware that any breach of this undertaking shall render me liable to face strict disciplinary action by the University authorities, including the possibility of expulsion or any other legal proceedings deemed appropriate by the competent authority or this Honourable Court.” Instant petition stands disposed of in the above terms. However, the University administration will not be prejudiced by any interim order passed by this Court and the students/petitioners will not be treated adversely in future. JUDGE JUDGE *Abdullah Channa/PS*
